Grandiloquence.text: "grand" (great) + "loqu" (speak) = speaking in a massive, overly pompous way.

Gratuitous.text: Gratuity - adding an unnecessary, uncalled-for extra tip when the service was already bad.

Gregarious.text: "greg" (flock) = loving to be part of the flock; highly sociable and outgoing.

Grievous.text: Grief - an injury or mistake so severe that it causes immense grief.

Guile.text: Disguise - a criminal using a clever disguise out of pure guile and trickery.

Hackneyed.text: From Hackney, an area in London where horses were bred to be rented out. The horses became so overworked and common that the word evolved…

Hallowed.text: Halloween (All Hallows' Eve) - referring to the most holy, deeply respected evening.

Harangue.text: Hear anger - a long, aggressive speech where all you hear is the speaker's intense anger.

Hardy.text: Hard - a plant or person tough enough to survive extremely hard, freezing conditions.

Harrowing.text: Arrowing - being shot at by a rain of flying arrows is a terrifying, highly distressing experience.

Haughty.text: High - acting incredibly high and mighty, looking down on everyone else.

Hedonist.text: He done it - "He done it" just for the sheer thrill, because a hedonist lives strictly for physical pleasure.

Hegemony.text: Huge money - the empire with huge money has absolute dominance and control over the weaker nations.

Heinous.text: Hate us - committing crimes so terribly wicked they make the entire world hate us.

Heterogeneous.text: "hetero" (different) + "gen" (kind) = made up of completely different, varied parts.

Hiatus.text: Hide us - the famous band begging "hide us" so they can take a long, badly needed break from the public.

Iconoclast.text: "icon" (image) + "clast" (breaker) = a rebel who attacks and breaks heavily established traditions and beliefs.

Idiosyncratic.text: "idio" (peculiar/self) = a highly unique, strange trait specific to one individual.

Idolatrous.text: "idol" = worshiping an idol or a person to an excessive, blind degree.

Ignominious.text: "ig-" (not) + "nomin" (name) = having your good name totally destroyed; suffering a disgraceful public defeat.

Illicit.text: Ill - doing highly illegal things that make society ill.

Immutable.text: "im-" (not) + "mut" (change) = a law of physics that is entirely unable to be mutated or changed.

Impassive.text: "im-" (not) + "pass" (feeling) = an expression showing absolutely zero emotion or feeling.

Impeccable.text: "im-" (not) + "pecc" (sin) = a performance or outfit that is entirely without sin or flaw.

Imperious.text: Emperor - acting like a bossy, highly arrogant emperor barking orders.

Impertinent.text: "im-" (not) + "pertinent" (proper/relevant) = acting outside the bounds of proper respect; incredibly rude to an authority figure.

Impervious.text: "im-" (not) + "per" (through) + "via" (way) = providing no way through; a raincoat impervious to water.

Impetuous.text: Impulse - acting strictly on wild impulse without thinking of the consequences.

Impinge.text: Pinch - encroaching on someone's personal space until you literally pinch them.

Implacable.text: "im-" (not) + "plac" (calm/please) = an enemy whose raging anger is impossible to calm down.

Impregnable.text: "im-" (not) + "pregnable" (vulnerable to capture) = a heavy stone fortress that is impossible to break into.

Impudent.text: Rude student - a highly bold, shamelessly disrespectful student backtalking the principal.

Impute.text: Put - to put the blame for the mistake directly onto someone else's record.

Inane.text: Insane - a comment so painfully empty and silly it borders on insane.

Inarticulate.text: "in-" (not) + "articulate" = entirely unable to speak clearly or express thoughts.

Incarnate.text: "in-" (in) + "carn" (flesh) = a spirit or idea given physical, bodily form in the flesh.

Incendiary.text: "incend" (fire) = a bomb or a speech specifically designed to cause massive fires or conflict.

Incessant.text: "in-" (not) + "cess" (stop) = a dripping faucet that is never stopping.

Inchoate.text: Inches - a business plan that is only a few inches along; just barely starting and completely unformed.

Incisive.text: "in-" (into) + "caedere" (cut) = mental analysis that cuts straight to the heart of the matter; razor-sharp.

Incontrovertible.text: "in-" (not) + "controvert" (dispute) = DNA evidence that is entirely impossible to argue against.

Incorrigible.text: "in-" (not) + "corrigere" (correct) = a criminal so deeply bad they are completely beyond correction.

Incumbent.text: Occupies the seat now - the officeholder already in power (the one whose role is filled).

Indefatigable.text: "in-" (not) + "defatigate" (tire) = a marathon runner whose energy is impossible to tire out.

Indigent.text: Dig in dirt - someone so desperately poor they have to dig in the dirt to find food.

Indignation.text: "in-" (not) + "dign" (worthy) = fierce anger sparked by being treated in a completely unworthy or unfair way.

Indolent.text: Indoor tent - laying lazily in an indoor tent all day doing absolutely nothing.

Indomitable.text: "in-" (not) + "domit" (tame) = an underdog spirit that is impossible to crush or tame.

Ineffable.text: "in-" (not) + "effable" (speakable) = looking at the Grand Canyon and feeling an awe so immense it cannot be expressed in words.

Inept.text: "in-" (not) + "apt" (skilled) = entirely clumsy and lacking any skill for the job.

Inexorable.text: "in-" (not) + "exorare" (persuade) = the marching army was impossible to persuade or stop.

Inextricable.text: "in-" (not) + "extricate" (untangle) = two headphones hopelessly and impossibly tangled together.

Infamy.text: "in-" (not) + "fame" = possessing world fame, but strictly for highly evil reasons.

Ingenuous.text: Genuine - someone with a highly innocent, trusting, and completely genuine nature.

Inimical.text: "in-" (not) + "amicus" (friend) = deeply unfriendly, hostile, and actively harmful.

Iniquity.text: "in-" (not) + "equ" (fair/equal) = a situation of gross injustice or deeply wicked behavior.

Innate.text: "in-" (in) + "nat" (born) = a perfect pitch musical talent you were naturally born with.

Insatiable.text: "in-" (not) + "satiable" (satisfied) = a hunger for power that is absolutely impossible to satisfy.

Insidious.text: Inside us - a dangerous, sneaky disease spreading quietly inside us without being noticed until it's too late.

Insipid.text: Sip it - the tea is so totally flavorless and boring you can barely force yourself to sip it.

Insolent.text: Insulting - a teenager showing an incredibly rude and insulting lack of respect to a judge.

Insular.text: "insula" (island) = having a highly isolated and narrow-minded worldview, living purely on a cultural island.

Insurgent.text: "in-" (against) + "surgere" (rise) = rebels rising up violently against the established government.

Interject.text: "inter" (between) + "ject" (throw) = sharply throwing your own comment between other speakers' words.

Interlocutor.text: "inter" (between) + "loc" (speak) = the specific person you are speaking back and forth with in a conversation.

Interminable.text: "in-" (not) + "termin" (end) = a boring university lecture that feels completely endless.

Intimation.text: Intimate - quietly sharing a very subtle, intimate hint rather than a direct statement.

Intractable.text: "in-" (not) + "tract" (pull/manage) = a highly stubborn problem or person that is utterly unmanageable.

Intransigent.text: Transfer - a negotiator aggressively refusing to transfer or compromise their original position.

Intrepid.text: "in-" (not) + "trepidus" (alarmed) = an explorer who is completely fearless and undaunted by danger.

Inundate.text: "in-" (into) + "unda" (wave) = being completely flooded by a massive wave of emails or tasks.

Inure.text: In your - the freezing cold has been in your life for so long you've grown completely numb and used to it.

Invective.text: Infective - highly abusive, toxic language that spreads through a crowd like an infection.

Inveterate.text: "in-" (in) + "veter" (old) = an extremely old, deeply established habit you cannot break.

Inviolable.text: "in-" (not) + "violable" = an absolute rule that is entirely safe from being violated or broken.

Irascible.text: Erasable - a teacher with a terrible temper who violently snaps when you use an un-erasable pen on the whiteboard.

Irreverence.text: "ir-" (not) + "revere" (respect) = laughing out loud at a funeral; showing a complete lack of proper respect.

Irrevocable.text: "ir-" (not) + "re" (back) + "voc" (call) = a final decision that can absolutely never be called back.

Jubilant.text: Jubilee - a massive, roaring celebration full of intense joy.

Judicious.text: "jud" (judge) = a leader possessing incredibly good, careful judgment.

Knell.text: Bell - the incredibly slow, solemn ringing of a funeral bell.

Laconic.text: From Laconia, the region of ancient Sparta. The Spartans were highly famous for giving incredibly brief, blunt, and tough answers to their…

Languid.text: Language - speaking in a highly slow, tired, and lazy language because you have zero energy.

Largess.text: Large - a highly generous billionaire handing out large sums of cash to charity.

Latent.text: Late tent - the true talent is hiding asleep in the tent, waiting to emerge late in the game.

Laudatory.text: "laud" (praise) = a speech expressing intense, glowing praise for a hero.

Legerdemain.text: From the French phrase léger de main meaning "light of hand." It is exactly the skill a magician or pickpocket uses for sleight of hand…

Licentious.text: License - acting recklessly, as if you have a government license to break all moral and sexual rules.

Limpid.text: Liquid - a mountain stream that is completely clear, pure, and see-through liquid.

Lucid.text: "luc" (light) = a complex argument suddenly made completely clear and brilliantly illuminated.

Lurid.text: Lure in - a tabloid using violently graphic, shocking headlines strictly to lure readers in.

Magnanimous.text: "magn" (great) + "anim" (spirit) = a leader with a massive, highly forgiving spirit toward defeated enemies.

Malediction.text: "mal" (bad) + "dict" (speak) = a witch speaking a dark, terrible curse over a village.

Malevolent.text: "mal" (bad) + "vol" (wish) = heavily wishing severe evil and harm on others.

Malleable.text: Mallet - a metal that is soft and flexible enough to be easily shaped by a mallet.

Manifold.text: "mani" (many) + "fold" = having many different, highly diverse folds or forms.

Maudlin.text: Model in - a model in a cheap soap opera crying excessively and artificially over a tiny broken nail.

Maverick.text: Named after Samuel Maverick, an 1800s Texas rancher who famously refused to brand his cattle. It completely evolved to mean any…

Mawkish.text: Ma kiss - your Ma aggressively kissing your cheek in front of your high school friends; overly sentimental and sickening.

Maxim.text: Maximum - a highly condensed, short phrase carrying the absolute maximum amount of truth.

Mendacious.text: Mend - having to desperately mend your story because you were caught being highly deceitful and lying.

Mercurial.text: Derived from the Roman god Mercury (and the liquid metal). Describes someone prone to incredibly rapid, unpredictable, and explosive mood…

Meritorious.text: "merit" (deserve) = an action highly deserving of intense praise and reward.

Metamorphosis.text: "meta" (change) + "morph" (shape) = a caterpillar undergoing a complete physical transformation into a butterfly.

Modicum.text: Modest crumb - having only a tiny, extremely modest crumb of respect left for someone.

Mollify.text: "moll" (soft) + "fy" (make) = to completely soften someone's raging anger with an apology.

Morass.text: More mass - every step sinks deeper; a hopeless mix of mud, mess, and delay.

Mores.text: Morals - the absolutely fundamental moral customs and unwritten rules of a group.

Morose.text: No rose - being incredibly depressed, sullen, and gloomy because you got no rose on Valentine's Day.

Multifarious.text: "multi" (many) = a museum collection having many extremely diverse, varied parts.

Mundane.text: "mund" (world) = an ordinary, highly boring task related purely to the earthly world rather than the exciting heavens.

Munificence.text: "muni" (gift) + "fic" (make) = making incredibly generous, massive financial gifts.

Mutable.text: "mut" (change) = a situation that is highly prone to constant change.

Myriad.text: Pyramid - a myriad of heavy stone blocks were used to build the massive pyramid (meaning countless or extremely high in number).

Nadir.text: Nada - hitting absolute rock bottom where you have completely nada (nothing) left.

Nascent.text: New scent - a brand new born project having a nascent, totally fresh scent.

Nebulous.text: "nebul" (cloud) = an idea that is highly cloudy, vague, and undefined.

Nefarious.text: No fairness - an utterly evil villain acting with absolutely zero fairness.

Neophyte.text: "neo" (new) + "phyt" (plant) = a newly planted convert; an absolute, raw beginner.

Nocturnal.text: "noct" (night) = a predator highly active only in the dead of night.

Noisome.text: Nose - an odor so absolutely foul and toxic it physically attacks your nose.

Nominal.text: "nomin" (name) = the king holds power in name only; the actual amount of control is incredibly tiny.

Noxious.text: "nox" (harm) = highly toxic, poisonous, and physically harmful gas.

Obdurate.text: "ob" (against) + "dur" (hard) = having a completely hardened heart against any persuasion or pity.

Obfuscate.text: "ob" (against) + "fuscare" (darken) = to completely darken or hide the truth to confuse the public.

Oblique.text: Ob-look - taking an indirect, highly slanted look at the problem rather than attacking it straight on.

Obsequious.text: Obsessed yes - fawning on authority, agreeing endlessly to stay in the boss's good graces.

Obstreperous.text: Street prep - a loud, completely unruly crowd causing wild chaos while prepping in the street.

Odious.text: Odor - a personality or crime so deeply repulsive it hits you like a foul, sickening odor.

Officious.text: Office boss - an annoying, low-level coworker acting exactly like the office boss and aggressively meddling in your work.

Ominous.text: Omen - dark storm clouds giving a threatening omen that something very bad is coming.

Onerous.text: On us - an incredibly heavy, punishingly difficult burden placed squarely on us.

Opulent.text: Opal - a massive mansion dripping in opals, diamonds, and sheer, excessive wealth.

Orthodox.text: "ortho" (straight/correct) + "dox" (belief) = following the strictly established, completely traditional beliefs.

Ostensible.text: Sensible - presenting a sensible-sounding, fake excuse that completely hides your true motive.

Ostentatious.text: Ostrich - walking around in an enormous ostrich-feather hat specifically to aggressively show off your wealth.

Ostracism.text: From ancient Athens, where citizens voted to banish dangerous politicians from the city by writing their names on pieces of broken pottery…

Pacific.text: "pac" (peace) = a demeanor that is deeply calm, tranquil, and peaceful (like the Pacific Ocean).

Palatable.text: Plate - the strange food on the plate is finally agreeable and okay to your taste.

Palliate.text: Pill ate - you ate a pill to soothe and hide the severe symptoms, even though it didn't actually cure the disease.

Pallid.text: Pale - a completely pale, sickly, and utterly lifeless face.

Panacea.text: "pan" (all) + "akos" (cure) = a mythical, highly sought-after cure-all for absolutely every disease.

Paramount.text: Mountain - sitting at the absolute highest, most supreme mountain peak of importance.

Pariah.text: Mariah - Mariah acted so wildly that the entire town exiled her as a total social outcast.

Parsimony.text: Purse money - tightly and fiercely guarding your purse money because you are incredibly cheap.

Pathos.text: "path" (feeling) = an artistic quality that successfully evokes incredibly deep pity or sadness in the audience.

Paucity.text: "pauc" (few) = an area suffering from a severe, highly dangerous shortage of resources.

Pejorative.text: Major dive - an insulting, highly negative word that makes your public reputation take a major dive.

Pellucid.text: "per" (through) + "luc" (light) = water or writing letting maximum light through; perfectly clear and easy to understand.

Penchant.text: Pendant - wearing a specific, unique pendant every day because you have a massive, natural liking for it.

Penitent.text: Penalty tent - sitting alone in the penalty tent feeling incredibly sorry and guilty for your sins.

Penultimate.text: "paene" (almost) + "ultimus" (last) = the item that is exactly second to last.

Penurious.text: Penny furious - a guy who becomes furious if he loses even a single penny because he is desperately, severely poor.

Perfidious.text: "per" (through/away) + "fid" (trust) = entirely breaking your faith; highly treacherous and backstabbing.

Perfunctory.text: "per" (through) + "funct" (perform) = performing a daily task just to get through it, with absolutely zero actual interest or care.

Permeate.text: "per" (through) + "meare" (pass) = the smell of smoke managing to pass completely through every part of a room.

Pernicious.text: "per" (completely) + "nec" (death) = spreading a highly dangerous, quiet, and deadly harm.

Perspicacity.text: "per" (through) + "spec" (look) = looking straight through the noise with incredibly razor-sharp mental insight.

Pertinacious.text: Persistent - fiercely and stubbornly holding onto an opinion with massive persistence.

Pervasive.text: "per" (through) + "vadere" (go) = an idea spreading rapidly and going into absolutely every corner of society.

Petulance.text: Pet - acting exactly like a moody, highly childish pet throwing a wild tantrum.

Philanthropic.text: "phil" (love) + "anthrop" (human) = giving massive wealth away entirely because of a deep love for humanity.

Phlegmatic.text: From the ancient medical theory of the four humors. Doctors believed having too much "phlegm" in your body meant you had a completely…

Pithy.text: Pity - it's a pity his speech was so incredibly short, but it was highly dense and effective.

Pittance.text: Pity cents - a wage so insultingly tiny they just throw you a few extra cents out of sheer pity.

Placate.text: Play date - setting up a highly fun play date to completely calm down a raging, angry toddler.

Placid.text: Plastic - an ocean surface as completely smooth, calm, and unchanging as solid plastic.

Platitude.text: Flat attitude - offering a flat, highly overused cliché instead of real, helpful advice.

Plaudits.text: "plaudere" (clap) = receiving heavy, wildly enthusiastic applause and praise from the crowd.

Plenitude.text: "plen" (full) = a completely full, massive abundance of food.
